methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

private.name: Steady Energy

data.narrative:
44.01 is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $37M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) with valuations ranging from $110M to $1.8B, plus a single public comparable.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $74M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables produces a median valuation of $175M, with a range floor of $110M and ceiling of $1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the peer group—notably Watershed's $1.8B valuation—constraining the precision of median-based inference.

The triangulated range across both methods is $63M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $124M. The $74M stage-progression result anchors the lower bound, while peer median establishes the midpoint and upper zone. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer median given the outlier presence of Watershed, and the absence of robust public market data. A valuation in the $100M–$160M range appears most consistent with the available evidence, though the wide peer dispersion warrants sensitivity to capital efficiency and stage-specific risk factors.
